resources.string: Runs USSD checks in the foreground
USSD behavior depends on your carrier and Android version. Many networks need one stacked code per step or manual confirmation. Storing a PIN on the device is risky—use only on a phone you control.
Service PIN (USSD digits; defaults to 9900 if left empty)
Sarif Auto can type your service PIN into the system USSD window after *222#, because the phone API often does not accept a second PIN-only USSD request. Enable only if you trust this app.
Turn on the switch above, then enable “Sarif Auto” under Downloaded apps in Accessibility.
Balance USSD (one per line). First *222#, then Service PIN is sent as digits only in a second request after “Enter PIN” (not *222*PIN#).
Phone and notification permissions are required for USSD and the foreground notification.
Monitor started. Check the persistent notification — USSD may not show a popup.
Add at least one balance USSD line (or restore defaults) before starting.
Cannot start background service from here. Open the app from the launcher and try again.
No money amount found in the USSD reply — adjust steps or parser for your carrier.
Parsed balance %1$s — running send steps if any.
Cycle done — waiting for next interval.
USSD failed (permission or network). Confirm CALL_PHONE is allowed.
